name: mkdocs-gallery

metadata.name: mkdocs-gallery
metadata.summary: a `mkdocs` plugin to generate example galleries from python scripts, similar to `sphinx-gallery`.

documentation:
# mkdocs-gallery

*[Sphinx-Gallery](https://sphinx-gallery.github.io/) features for [mkdocs](https://www.mkdocs.org/) (no [Sphinx](sphinx-doc.org/) dependency !).*

[![Python versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/mkdocs-gallery.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/mkdocs-gallery/) [![Build Status](https://github.com/smarie/mkdocs-gallery/actions/workflows/base.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/smarie/mkdocs-gallery/actions/workflows/base.yml) [![Tests Status](https://smarie.github.io/mkdocs-gallery/reports/junit/junit-badge.svg?dummy=8484744)](https://smarie.github.io/mkdocs-gallery/reports/junit/report.html) [![Coverage Status](https://smarie.github.io/mkdocs-gallery/reports/coverage/coverage-badge.svg?dummy=8484744)](https://smarie.github.io/mkdocs-gallery/reports/coverage/index.html) [![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/smarie/mkdocs-gallery/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/smarie/mkdocs-gallery) [![Flake8 Status](https://smarie.github.io/mkdocs-gallery/reports/flake8/flake8-badge.svg?dummy=8484744)](https://smarie.github.io/mkdocs-gallery/reports/flake8/index.html)

[![Documentation](https://img.shields.io/badge/doc-latest-blue.svg)](https://smarie.github.io/mkdocs-gallery/) [![PyPI](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/mkdocs-gallery.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/mkdocs-gallery/) [![Downloads](https://pepy.tech/badge/mkdocs-gallery)](https://pepy.tech/project/mkdocs-gallery) [![Downloads per week](https://pepy.tech/badge/mkdocs-gallery/week)](https://pepy.tech/project/mkdocs-gallery) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/smarie/mkdocs-gallery.svg)](https://github.com/smarie/mkdocs-gallery/stargazers) [![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/DOI/10.5281/zenodo.5786851.svg)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.5786851)

Do you love [Sphinx-Gallery](https://sphinx-gallery.github.io/) but prefer [mkdocs](https://www.mkdocs.org/) over [Sphinx](sphinx-doc.org/) for your documentation ? `mkdocs-gallery` was written for you ;) 

It relies on [mkdocs-material](https://squidfunk.github.io/mkdocs-material) to get the most of mkdocs, so that your galleries look nice!

The documentation for users is available here: [https://smarie.github.io/mkdocs-gallery/](https://smarie.github.io/mkdocs-gallery/)

A readme for developers is available here: [https://github.com/smarie/mkdocs-gallery](https://github.com/smarie/mkdocs-gallery)

The base class of all errors in this plugin.

See https://www.mkdocs.org/dev-guide/plugins/#handling-errors.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.notebook.jupyter_notebook_skeleton
documentation: Returns a dictionary with the elements of a Jupyter notebook

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.notebook.rst2md
documentation:
Converts the RST text from the examples docstrings and comments
into markdown text for the Jupyter notebooks

# Parameters

text: str

: RST input to be converted to MD

gallery_conf

: The mkdocs-gallery configuration dictionary.

target_dir

: Path that notebook is intended for. Used where relative paths
  may be required.

heading_levels: dict

: Mapping of heading style `(over_char, under_char)` to heading level.
  Note that `over_char` is `None` when only underline is present.

Scales an image with the same aspect ratio centered in an

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.utils.optipng
documentation:
Optimize a PNG in place.

# Parameters

file

: The file. If it ends with '.png', `optipng -o7 fname` will
  be run. If it fails because the `optipng` executable is not found
  or optipng fails, the function returns.

args

: Extra command-line arguments, such as `['-o7']`.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.utils.replace_ext
documentation:
Replace the extension in `file` with `new_ext`, with optional initial `expected_ext` check.

Parameters
----------
file : Path
    the file path.

new_ext : str
    The new extension, e.g. '.ipynb'

expected_ext : str
    The expected original extension for checking, if provided.

Returns
-------
new_file : Path
    The same file with a different ext.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.utils.get_md5sum
documentation:
Returns md5sum of file

Parameters
----------
src_file : str
    Filename to get md5sum for.
mode : 't' or 'b'
    File mode to open file with. When in text mode, universal line endings
    are used to ensure consitency in hashes between platforms.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.utils._get_old_file
documentation: Return the same file without the .new suffix

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.utils._have_same_md5
documentation: Return `True` if both files have the same md5, computed using `mode`.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.utils._smart_move_md5
documentation:
Move `src_file` to `dst_file`, overwriting `dst_file` only if md5 has changed.

Parameters
----------

    The source file path.

dst_file : Path
    The destination file path.

md5_mode : str
    A string representing the md5 computation mode, 'b' or 't'

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.utils._new_file
documentation: Return the same file path with a .new additional extension.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.utils._replace_by_new_if_needed
documentation:
Use `file_new` (suffix .new) instead of the old file (same path but no suffix).

If the new file is identical to the old one, the old one will not be touched.

Parameters
----------
file_new : Path
    The new file, ending with .new suffix.

md5_mode : str
    A string representing the md5 computation mode, 'b' or 't'

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.utils._smart_copy_md5
documentation:
Copy `src_file` to `dst_file`, overwriting `dst_file`, only if md5 has changed.

Parameters
----------
src_file : Path
    The source file path.

dst_file : Path
    The destination file path.

src_md5 : str
    If the source md5 was already computed, users may provide it here to avoid computing it again.

md5_mode : str
    A string representing the md5 computation mode, 'b' or 't'

Returns
-------
md5 : str
    The md5 of the file, if it has been provided or computed in the process, or None.

modules.name: mkdocs_gallery.scrapers
documentation:
# Scrapers for embedding images

Collect images that have been produced by code blocks.

The only scrapers we support are Matplotlib and Mayavi, others should
live in modules that will support them (e.g., PyVista, Plotly). Scraped
images are injected as rst `image-sg` directives into the `.md`
file generated for each example script.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.scrapers.matplotlib_scraper
documentation:
Scrape Matplotlib images.

# Parameters

block

: A tuple containing the (label, content, line_number) of the block.

script

: Dict of block variables.

kwargs

: Additional keyword arguments to pass to
  {meth}`~matplotlib.figure.Figure.savefig`, e.g. `format='svg'`.
  The `format` kwarg in particular is used to set the file extension
  of the output file (currently only 'png', 'jpg', and 'svg' are
  supported).

# Returns

md

: The Markdown that will be rendered to HTML containing
  the images. This is often produced by {func}`figure_md_or_html`.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.scrapers.mayavi_scraper
documentation:
Scrape Mayavi images.

# Parameters

block

: A tuple containing the (label, content, line_number) of the block.

script

: Script being run

# Returns

md

: The ReSTructuredText that will be rendered to HTML containing
  the images. This is often produced by {func}`figure_md_or_html`.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.scrapers.pyvista_scraper
documentation:
Scrape PyVista images.

# Parameters

block

: A tuple containing the (label, content, line_number) of the block.

script

: Script being run

# Returns

md

: The ReSTructuredText that will be rendered to HTML containing
  the images. This is often produced by {func}`figure_md_or_html`.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.scrapers._find_image_ext
documentation: Find an image, tolerant of different file extensions.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.scrapers.save_figures
documentation:
Save all open figures of the example code-block.

Parameters
----------
block : tuple
    A tuple containing the (label, content, line_number) of the block.

script : GalleryScript
    Script run.

Returns
-------
images_md : str
    md code to embed the images in the document.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.scrapers.figure_md_or_html
documentation:
Generate md or raw html for a list of image filenames.

Depending on whether we have one or more figures, we use a
single md call to 'image' or a horizontal list.

# Parameters

figure_paths

: List of strings of the figures' absolute paths.

sources_dir

: absolute path of Sphinx documentation sources

fig_titles

: Titles of figures, empty string if no titles found. Currently
  only supported for matplotlib figures, default = ''.

srcsetpaths

: List of dictionaries containing absolute paths. If
  empty, then srcset field is populated with the figure path.
  (see `image_srcset` configuration option). Otherwise,
  each dict is of the form
  {0: /images/image.png, 2.0: /images/image_2_0x.png}
  where the key is the multiplication factor and the contents
  the path to the image created above.

# Returns

images_md

: md code to embed the images in the document

The md code will have a custom `image-sg` directive that allows
multiple resolution images to be served e.g.:
\:srcset: /plot_types/imgs/img_001.png,

> /plot_types/imgs/img_2_0x.png 2.0x\`\`

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.scrapers._get_srcset_st
documentation:
Create the srcset string for including on the md line.
ie. sources_dir might be /home/sample-proj/source,
hinames posix paths to
0: /home/sample-proj/source/plot_types/images/img1.png,
2.0: /home/sample-proj/source/plot_types/images/img1_2_0x.png,
The result will be:
'/plot_types/basic/images/mkd_glr_pie_001.png,
/plot_types/basic/images/mkd_glr_pie_001_2_0x.png 2.0x'

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.scrapers.clean_modules
documentation:
Remove, unload, or reset modules after running each example.

After a script is executed it can load a variety of settings that one
does not want to influence in other examples in the gallery.

Parameters
----------
gallery_conf : dict
    The gallery configuration.

file : Path
    The example being run. Will be None when this is called entering
    a directory of examples to be built.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.binder.gen_binder_url
documentation:
Generate a Binder URL according to the configuration in conf.py.

Parameters
----------
script: GalleryScript
    The script for which a Binder badge will be generated.
binder_conf: dict or None
    The Binder configuration dictionary. See `gen_binder_md` for details.

Returns
-------
binder_url : str
    A URL that can be used to direct the user to the live Binder environment.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.binder.gen_binder_md
documentation:
Generate the MD + link for the Binder badge.

Parameters
----------
script: GalleryScript
    The script for which a Binder badge will be generated.

binder_conf: dict or None
    If a dictionary it must have the following keys:

    'binderhub_url'
        The URL of the BinderHub instance that's running a Binder service.
    'org'
        The GitHub organization to which the documentation will be pushed.
    'repo'
        The GitHub repository to which the documentation will be pushed.
    'branch'
        The Git branch on which the documentation exists (e.g., gh-pages).
    'dependencies'
        A list of paths to dependency files that match the Binderspec.

Returns
-------
md : str
    The Markdown for the Binder badge that links to this file.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.binder.copy_binder_files
documentation: Copy all Binder requirements and notebooks files.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.binder._copy_binder_reqs
documentation:
Copy Binder requirements files to a ".binder" folder in the docs.

See https://mybinder.readthedocs.io/en/latest/using/config_files.html#config-files

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.binder._remove_ipynb_files
documentation:
Given a list of files in `contents`, remove all files named `ipynb` or
directories named `images` and return the result.

Used with the `shutil` "ignore" keyword to filter out non-ipynb files.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.binder._copy_binder_notebooks
documentation:
Copy Jupyter notebooks to the binder notebooks directory.

Copy each output gallery directory structure but only including the
Jupyter notebook files.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.binder.check_binder_conf
documentation: Check to make sure that the Binder configuration is correct.

Finds the longest form of variable names and their imports in code.

Only retains names from imported modules.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.py_source_parser.parse_source_file
documentation:
Parse source file into AST node.

Parameters
----------
file : Path
    File path

Returns
-------
node : AST node
content : utf-8 encoded string

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.py_source_parser._get_docstring_and_rest
documentation:
Separate `filename` content between docstring and the rest.

Strongly inspired from ast.get_docstring.

# Parameters

file

: The source file

# Returns

docstring

: docstring of `filename`

rest

: `filename` content without the docstring

lineno

: The line number.

node

: The node.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.py_source_parser.extract_file_config
documentation: Pull out the file-specific config specified in the docstring.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.py_source_parser.split_code_and_text_blocks
documentation:
Return list with source file separated into code and text blocks.

# Parameters

source_file

: Path to the source file.

return_node

: If True, return the ast node.

# Returns

file_conf

: File-specific settings given in source file comments as:
  `# mkdocs_gallery_<name> = <value>`

blocks

: (label, content, line_number)
  List where each element is a tuple with the label ('text' or 'code'),
  the corresponding content string of block and the leading line number

node

: The parsed node.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.py_source_parser.remove_config_comments
documentation:
Return the content of *code_block* with in-file config comments removed.

Comment lines of the pattern '# mkdocs_gallery_[option] = [val]' are
removed, but surrounding empty lines are preserved.

Parameters
----------
code_block : str
    A code segment.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.downloads.python_zip
documentation:
Stores all files in file_list with modified extension `extension` into an zip file

Parameters
----------
file_list : List[Path]
    Holds all the files to be included in zip file. Note that if extension
    is set to

gallery : Gallery
    gallery for which to create the zip file

extension : str
    The replacement extension for files in file_list. '.py' or '.ipynb'.
    In order to deal with downloads of python sources and jupyter notebooks,
    since we know that there is one notebook for each python file.

Returns
-------
zipfile : Path
    zip file, written as `<target_dir>_{python,jupyter}.zip` depending on the extension

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.downloads.generate_zipfiles
documentation:
Collects all Python source files and Jupyter notebooks in
gallery_dir and makes zipfiles of them

Parameters
----------
gallery : Gallery
    path of the gallery to collect downloadable sources

Return
------
download_md: str
    Markdown to include download buttons to the generated files

Note that this image will be copied and possibly rescaled later to create the actual thumbnail.

classes.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_data_model.Gallery

methods.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_data_model.Gallery.__init__
documentation:
Parameters
----------
all_info : AllInformation
    The parent structure containing all configurations.

scripts_dir_rel_project : Path
    The source folder of the current gallery, containing the python files and the readme.md.
    For example ./docs/examples/. It must be relative to the project root.

generated_dir_rel_project : Path
    The target folder where the documents, notebooks and images from this sub-gallery should be generated.
    For example ./docs/generated/gallery (a subfolder of mkdocs src folder), or
    ./generated/gallery (not a subfolder of mkdocs src folder. TODO explicitly forbid this ?).
    It must be relative to the project root.

documentation: The folder where python scripts are located, as an absolute path.

documentation:
Path to this root gallery's index markdown page, relative to site root.
Note that subgalleries do not have such a page

documentation:
Collects script files to process in this gallery and sort them according to configuration.

Parameters
----------
recurse : bool
    If True, this will call collect_script_files on all subsections

apply_ignore_pattern : bool
    A boolean indicating if the 'ignore_pattern' gallery config option should be applied.

sort_files : bool
    A boolean indicating if the 'within_subsection_order' gallery config option should be applied.

Factory to create this object from the configuration.

modules.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single
documentation: Generator for a single script example in a gallery.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single.codestr2md
documentation: Return markdown code block from code string.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single._sanitize_md
documentation:
Use regex to remove at least some sphinx directives.

TODO is this still needed ?

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single.extract_readme_title
documentation:
Same as `extract_intro_and_title` for the readme files in galleries, but does not return the introduction.

Parameters
----------
file : Path
    The readme file path (used for error messages only).

contents : str
    The already parsed readme contents

Returns
-------
title : str
    The readme title

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single.extract_readme_last_subtitle
documentation:
Same as `extract_intro_and_title` for the readme files in galleries, but does not return the introduction.

Parameters
----------
file : Path
    The readme file path (used for error messages only).

contents : str
    The already parsed readme contents

Returns
-------
last_subtitle : str
    The readme last title, or None.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single.extract_intro_and_title
documentation:
Extract and clean the first paragraph of module-level docstring.

The title is not saved in the `script` object in this process, users have to do it explicitly.

Parameters
----------
docstring : str
    The docstring extracted from the top of the script.

script : GalleryScript
    The script where the docstring was extracted from (used for error messages only).

Returns
-------
title : str
    The title

introduction : str
    The introduction

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single.create_thumb_from_image
documentation:
Create a thumbnail image from the `src_image_path`.

Parameters
----------
script : GalleryScript
    The gallery script.

src_image_path : Path
    The source image path, with some flexibility about the extension.
    TODO do we actually need this flexibility here ?

Returns
-------
actual_thumb_file : Path
    The actual thumbnail file generated.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single.generate
documentation:
Generate the gallery md for an example directory, including the index.

Parameters
----------
gallery : GalleryBase
    The gallery or subgallery to process

seen_backrefs : Set
    Backrefs seen so far.

Returns
-------
title : str
    The gallery title, that is, the title of the readme file.

root_subtitle : str
    The gallery suptitle that will be used in case the gallery has subsections.

index_md : str
    The markdown to include in the global gallery readme.

results : List[GalleryScriptResults]
    A list of processing results for all scripts in this gallery.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single.handle_exception
documentation: Trim and format exception, maybe raise error, etc.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single.patch_warnings
documentation: Patch warnings.showwarning to actually write out the warning.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single._get_memory_base
documentation: Get the base amount of memory used by running a Python process.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single._ast_module
documentation:
Get ast.Module function, dealing with:
https://bugs.python.org/issue35894

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single._exec_and_get_memory
documentation: Execute ast, capturing output if last line is expression and get max memory usage.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single._get_last_repr
documentation:
Get a repr of the last expression, using first method in 'capture_repr'
available for the last expression.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single._get_code_output
documentation: Obtain standard output and html output in md.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single._reset_cwd_syspath
documentation: Reset current working directory to `cwd` and remove `path_to_remove` from `sys.path`.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single.execute_code_block
documentation:
Execute the code block of the example file.

Parameters
----------
compiler : codeop.Compile
    Compiler to compile AST of code block.

block : List[Tuple[str, str, int]]
    List of Tuples, each Tuple contains label ('text' or 'code'),
    the corresponding content string of block and the leading line number.

script: GalleryScript
    The gallery script

Returns
-------
code_output : str
    Output of executing code in md.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single.parse_and_execute
documentation:
Execute and capture output from python script already in block structure

Parameters
----------
script : GalleryScript
    The script

script_blocks : list
    (label, content, line_number)
    List where each element is a tuple with the label ('text' or 'code'),
    the corresponding content string of block and the leading line number

Returns
-------
output_blocks : list
    List of strings where each element is the restructured text
    representation of the output of each block

time_elapsed : float
    Time elapsed during execution

memory_used : float
    Memory used during execution

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single.generate_file_md
documentation:
Generate the md file for a given example.

Parameters
----------
script : GalleryScript
    The script to process

seen_backrefs : set
    The seen backreferences.

Returns
-------
result: FileResult
    The result of running this script

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single.generate_md_from_blocks
documentation:
Generate the md string containing the script prose, code and output.

# Parameters

script_blocks

: (label, content, line_number)
  List where each element is a tuple with the label ('text' or 'code'),
  the corresponding content string of block and the leading line number

output_blocks

: List of strings where each element is the restructured text
  representation of the output of each block

file_conf

: File-specific settings given in source file comments as:
  `# mkdocs_gallery_<name> = <value>`

gallery_conf

: Contains the configuration of mkdocs-gallery

# Returns

out

: The resulting markdown page.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_single.get_example_md_wrapper
documentation:
Creates the headers and footers for the example markdown. Returns a template

Parameters
----------
script : GalleryScript
    The script for which to generate the md.

time_elapsed : float
    Time elapsed in seconds while executing file

memory_used : float
    Additional memory used during the run.

Returns
-------
md_before : str
    Part of the final markdown that goes before the notebook / python script.

md_after : str
    Part of the final markdown that goes after the notebook / python script.

methods.name: mkdocs_gallery.plugin.MySubConfig.run_validation
documentation:
Fix SubConfig: errors and warnings were not caught

See https://github.com/mkdocs/mkdocs/pull/2710

Same as SubConfig except that it will be an empty dict when nothing is provided by user,
instead of a dict with all options containing their default values.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_gallery.generate_gallery_md
documentation:
Generate the Main examples gallery reStructuredText

Start the mkdocs-gallery configuration and recursively scan the examples
directories in order to populate the examples gallery

Returns
-------
md_files_toc : Dict[str, Tuple[str, Dict[str, str]]]
    A map of galleries src folders to title and galleries toc (map of title to path)

md_to_src_file : Dict[str, Path]
    A map of posix absolute file path to generated markdown example -> Path of the src file relative to project root

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_gallery.dict_to_list_of_dicts
documentation: Transform a dict containing several entries into a list of dicts containing one entry each (nav requirement)

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_gallery.fill_mkdocs_nav
documentation:
Creates a new nav by replacing all entries in the nav containing a reference to gallery_index

Parameters
----------
mkdocs_config

galleries_tocs : Dict[Path, Tuple[str, Dict[str, str]]]
    A reference dict containing for each gallery, its path (the key) and its title and contents. The
    contents is a dictionary containing title and path to md, for each element in the gallery.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_gallery._sec_to_readable
documentation: Convert a number of seconds to a more readable representation.

functions.name: mkdocs_gallery.gen_gallery.touch_empty_backreferences

Generate empty back-reference example files.

This avoids inclusion errors/warnings if there are no gallery
examples for a class / module that is being parsed by autodoc

Collects the list of falling examples and prints them with a traceback.

Raises ValueError if there where failing examples.
